value: Reduces memory usage by 26-54% (peak tokens) while preserving task performance; preserves >95% of accuracy when distilled into smaller compressors; enhances smaller LMs as long-horizon agents with up to 46% performance improvement.
Universal cross-framework agent knowledge base; smolagents up to 18.7pp gain at pass@3 (55.2%->73.9%); disagreement gate ensures retrieved knowledge enhances reasoning.
Open agentic framework that uses computers like a human; experience-augmented hierarchical planning + Agent-Computer Interface (ACI); 83.6% relative improvement on OSWorld benchmarks.
Induces commonly reused workflows from past experiences; flexibly applies to offline (training) and online (test-time) scenarios; substantial gains on web navigation benchmarks.
Self-evolving agent system: self-questioning for task generation, self-navigating for exploration via experience reuse, self-attributing for sample efficiency.

Proactive context management via folding inspired by retrospective consolidation; multi-scale folding from granular condensation to deep consolidation; superior performance without continual pretraining.
Agent-controlled memory operations as tool-based actions for long/short-term memory; trained via three-stage progressive RL with step-wise GRPO for sparse rewards.
Plan template extraction from completed agent runs; keyword retrieval; lightweight model adaptation; 50.31% cost reduction and 27.28% latency reduction.
Lifelong personalization of LLMs to continuously adapt to diverse user profiles; releases code and data for benchmarking lifelong personalized LLM systems.
Sparse attention mechanism using Hopfield-energy associative memory for token reconstruction; outperforms sparse Transformer baselines with fewer parameters; CVPR 2025.
Generalist agent with minimal predefinition and maximal self-evolution via MCP generation; 75.15% pass@1 on GAIA validation, 74.00% on Mathvista, 52.00% on PathVQA.
Self-evolution from generalist to domain expert via MCP synthesis, abstraction, and curation into MCP Box; 83.03% pass@1 and 89.09% pass@3 on GAIA; ~15% token cost reduction.
Locating-then-editing parametric edits via null-space projection of perturbations preserving original knowledge; boosts existing methods by 36.7% average across LLaMA3, GPT2-XL, GPT-J.
LLM agent builds and maintains memory graph integrating semantic and episodic information during exploration; outperforms memory baselines on text games and competitive on multi-hop QA.
Direct browser interaction via Playwright with predefined actions; explicit memory of key conclusions across steps; ~20% improvement over Search-R1 on multi-hop QA tasks.
Stores high-level thought-templates distilled from problem-solving; buffer-manager dynamically updates the meta-buffer; 11-51% improvements at 12% computational cost of competing methods.

Constructivist Agentic Memory (Piaget-inspired): structured schemata, flexible assimilation, dynamic accommodation; incremental overlapping clustering; balances performance and efficiency over QA, summarization, claim verification.

Comprehensive survey of continual learning of LLMs covering vertical (general->specific) and horizontal (across time/domains) continuity; structured around Continual Pre-Training, Domain-Adaptive Pre-training, and Continual Fine-Tuning.

Collaborative multi-agent framework for Windows UI automation; scenario-aware Task Scheduler decomposes tasks; per-agent memory units for self-evolution; interactive backtracking for human intervention.

Cognitive-inspired iterative retrieval with dynamic memory workspace; impasse detection -> probing queries -> evidence retrieval -> memory integration; up to 11% improvement over RAG on 200K+ token narratives.
Compresses past memories for long-range sequence learning; SOTA on WikiText-103 and Enwik8; introduces PG-19 benchmark; effective for speech and RL memory.
Disentangles abstract tool creation and concrete decision execution; LLMs autonomously generate custom tools using documentation and code; surpasses CoT and PoT on MATH and TabMWP.
Dynamic Structured Memory + Reasoning Tree using OWL-compliant KG over conversation history; 48% elevation in dialogue consistency, up to 10.1% quality on MT-Bench-101.
Self-improving AI iteratively modifies own code, validated through coding benchmarks; archive of generated agents; SWE-bench 20.0%->50.0%, Polyglot 14.2%->30.7%.
End-to-end deep reasoning agent integrating thinking, tool discovery, action execution; autonomous memory folding into episodic/working/tool memory; ToolPO RL training; gains across 8 benchmarks.
Persistent evolving memory of strategies, code snippets, problem-solving insights stored as concise transferable snippets; Claude AIME accuracy more than doubled; GPT-4o Game of 24 from 10% to 99%.
Mixture-of-LoRA for lifelong model editing with router network and continuous data-adapter mapping; outperforms 8 baselines on GPT-2 XL and LLaMA2-7B; AAAI-25.
Human episodic memory inspired LLM; events segmented online via Bayesian surprise + graph-theoretic refinement; outperforms InfLLM and RAG; retrieval across 10M tokens.

value: LLM agent extracts knowledge as natural language from training tasks without parameter updates; in-context learning over accumulated experiences; AAAI 2024.

LLM multi-agent framework for financial decisions with manager-analyst hierarchy and conceptual verbal reinforcement; episodic self-critique updates investment beliefs; selective propagation reduces communication cost.

Gradient-free continual evolution of LLM agents via structured experience library through reflection on success/failure; AIME25 +23%, USPTO50k +10%, ProteinGym +14%; identifies scaling laws of experiential growth.
Forgetting-curve-inspired memory replay for continual LLM learning; model-centric notion of time via optimizer update magnitude; intensity-aware regularization; tested on 0.6B-13B LMs.
Hierarchical agentic memory for multi-agent systems with three-tier graph (insight/query/interaction); bi-directional traversal; +20.89% embodied success and +10.12% knowledge QA across 5 benchmarks.
Heavy-Hitter Oracle for KV-cache eviction balancing recent and high-impact tokens; up to 29x throughput, 1.9x latency reduction with 20% heavy hitters retained on OPT/LLaMA/GPT-NeoX.
Hierarchical Hindsight Reflection separates high-level planning memory from low-level execution memory; refined continuously through distillation; outperforms ExpeL on two benchmarks.
Agent Skill Induction (ASI) on the fly via program-based skills with verification; +23.5% over static baseline, +11.3% over text-skill, 10.7-15.3% fewer action steps on WebArena.

Iterative paradigm for long-horizon research agents using interaction scaling and evolving report as persistent memory; +14.5pp avg over open agents; gains 3.5%-42.5% extending to 2048 interactions; ICLR 2026.
Open-world Minecraft agent combining multimodal LM with multimodal memory of pre-trained knowledge and game survival experiences; completes 200+ tasks; 5x reliability over SOTA on diamond pickaxe.

Real-time LLM personalization without parameter updates by extracting personalized factual triples and optimizing user-specific KGs; reduces compute by avoiding back-propagation; tested on GPT-2/Llama2/Llama3.
Human-demonstration-based mobile GUI agent with DemoParser/KnowSeeker/ActExecutor; LearnGUI dataset; Gemini-1.5-Pro 19.3%->51.7%, UI-TARS-7B-SFT 18.1%->32.8% online.

Modular procedural memory framework for multi-agent LLM workflow automation; decomposes trajectories into reusable units distributed across orchestrator/agents; orchestrator memory aids decomposition, agent memory aids execution.
Sensory/short-term/long-term human-cognition memory system; QA gains up to 7.7% (GPT) and 29.3% (Qwen); token usage reduced 38x/20.9x; API calls reduced 30x/55.5x; offline sleep-time consolidation.
Decoder-only Transformer with auxiliary memory module via cross-attention and gating; +86.3% on BABILong, +5.0% on MMLU; preserves general-purpose capabilities.
Frozen backbone LLM + adaptive residual side-network as memory retriever/reader; ~65K token memory bank; outperforms long-context baselines on ChapterBreak; in-context learning gains.
Real-time generative agents with option-action framework, asynchronous self-monitoring, summarize-and-forget memory; 10-100x lower computational cost; LyfeGame autonomous social reasoning.

Long-term memory mechanism with co-trained retriever extending MemoryLLM from <20K to >160K tokens; latent-space memory pool of 1B parameters compressing past info into hidden states across all layers.
Multi-graph memory for long-horizon reasoning across semantic/temporal/causal/entity graphs; policy-guided traversal; outperforms SOTA on LoCoMo and LongMemEval; ACL 2026.
RL framework for agents with core/episodic/semantic memory; trained to decide what to store/structure/update; trained on 30K, generalizes to 400K+ tokens (13x training length).

RL framework for constant memory across long multi-turn interactions via shared internal state; 3.5x performance improvement and 3.7x memory reduction vs Qwen2.5-14B-Instruct on 16-objective multi-hop QA.
Agent workflow processing long documents in segments with overwrite-based memory; extends DAPO for multi-conversation training; 32K-trained extends to 3.5M with <5% loss; 95%+ on 512K RULER.

Stateful memory-augmented BART for efficient dialogue modeling; separate memory module alongside pre-trained transformer enables information exchange between memory states and current input contexts; tested on 3 dialogue + 2 LM datasets.
Memory-based online RL via M-MDP with episodic memory and case-selection policy; no LLM fine-tuning; 87.88% Pass@3 on GAIA validation; 4.7-9.6% OOD improvements.

Meta-evolution of agent memory architecture itself, not just experiential knowledge; SmolAgent and Flash-Searcher gains up to 17.06%; EvolveLab self-evolving memory codebase with encode/store/retrieve/manage modules.
External dynamic memory transformer for sequence modeling; 8.1x less memory and 3.2x faster inference; memory replay back-propagation (MRBP) enables long-range BPTT.

Memory-augmented transformers (Memformers) can implement linear first-order optimization methods (gradient descent, conjugate gradient, momentum); memory registers store past gradients and intermediate attention values; mixture-of-experts framework adapts learned methods to OOD scenarios.

Generative latent memory framework for self-evolving agents with Memory Trigger and Memory Weaver; surpasses ExpeL/AWM by up to 38.22%; spontaneously develops planning/procedural/working memory faculties.
Intent-driven memory selection: intent-aligned retrieval + missing-slot guided filtering with LLaMA-8B re-ranker; on MS-TOD benchmark 88%->99% task success and -2.84 turns.
